title: Reviving chickpea through Integrated Pest Management in Nepal
abstract:
Chickpea (Cicer arietinum L.) is one of the most
important grain legumes in Nepal. It is a major
source of protein and forms an integral part of
the Nepalese diet. Constraints to chickpea,
however, include the disease botrytis grey mold
(BGM) and the pod borer insect (Helicoverpa
armigera). The result was a sharp decline in
chickpea production due to increasing losses,
even up to 100%.
